incorrects.description: Student selected 6, counted marks instead of using scale of 2

steps.text: You counted the marks. Each mark stands for 2 students, not 1. Count by 2s along the Soccer bar.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, read axis incorrectly or estimated

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The Soccer bar goes all the way to the 12 mark. The scale is 2, so the last mark the bar touches is 12.

incorrects.description: Student selected 24, multiplied 12 by 2 instead of reading the value

steps.text: Here's a hint: the scale of 2 tells us how to read the axis. It doesn't mean we multiply the bar's value. Where does the Soccer bar end?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Soccer bar traces to axis, landing on 12

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The Soccer bar reaches the 12 mark on the axis. With a scale of 2, the numbers go 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The bar ends at 12, so 12 students chose soccer.
